content.text: Answer the following problems and show all your work. Support all statements to the best of your ability.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Let \(X\) be a connected normal space having two distinct points \(x\) and \(y\). Show that \(X\) has uncountably many points.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: State the Brouwer fixed-point theorem for the disc \(B^2\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Let \(A\) be a 3 by 3 matrix of positive real numbers. Show that \(A\) has a positive real eigenvalue. Hint: Let \(B\) be the intersection of the unit sphere \(S^2\) and the positive octant, \(\{(x,y,z)\in\mathbb{R}^3\mid x,y,z\geq 0\}\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show the images of the generators of the fundamental groups of the circles do not generate the fundamental group of \(H\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Compute the fundamental group of the connected sum of a torus and second torus \(T^2\#T^2\).

content.text: Answer the following with complete definitions or statements or short proofs.

body.text: Show that \(\mathbb{R}^2\) is not homeomorphic to \(\mathbb{R}^3\).
If \(j:A\hookrightarrow X\) is the inclusion of a retract, then show that the induced map on fundamental groups \(j_\#\) is injective.
What is the fundamental group of the torus \(T^2=S^1\times S^1\)?
Given continuous maps of topological spaces \(f:A\to X\) and \(g:A\to Y\), state the universal property satisfied by the pushout.
